commit.message:
fix: enable dr_flac native CRC checking, remove manual frame verification

Remove DR_FLAC_NO_CRC to let dr_flac handle corrupt frame detection
natively via CRC-8 header and CRC-16 data verification. Bad frames
are automatically skipped with resync to the next valid frame.

This eliminates ~200 lines of manual frame boundary detection and
CRC post-processing that was prone to false positives.

commit.message:
fix: replace require() as {} with destructuring annotations for Node v22 compat

Node v22's --experimental-strip-types cannot handle `as` type assertions
(only strips annotations). Replace all 163 `require('...') as { ... }`
patterns with `}: { ... } = require('...')` destructuring annotations
across 38 files. Purely mechanical transformation — no logic changes.

Fixes syntax errors on Ubuntu with Node v22.17.0.

config: 최소 재실 시간 120초 → 30초로 조정

ENTRY 후 120초간 펄스+침묵 EXIT 차단으로 인해
게이트 통과 후 2분이 지나야 EXIT 처리되던 문제.

30초로 단축 시 퇴실 감지: 30초(차단) + 45초(침묵) = 최대 75초 이내.
실제 장시간 근무 후 퇴실은 차단 시간이 이미 지나 45초 이내 감지.

commit.message:
Switch MARVIN streaming to native Lambda Function URL (zip, no Docker)

Replace Docker/Web Adapter approach with a self-contained zip-based Lambda
that uses native response streaming (InvokeMode: RESPONSE_STREAM). The
marvin-stream/ directory contains handler.py with all MARVIN logic inline,
its own requirements.txt, and a copy of marvin-knowledge.md. Reverts
Dockerfile to original (PDF-only). Updates SAM template to Runtime: python3.12.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite hydration as server-side SQL for performance (#101)

Replace the slow TypeScript parse loop (pull JSON, parse locally, push
rows back) with a single INSERT ... SELECT that runs entirely in
MotherDuck. JSON extraction, aggregation, minutes summing, and starter
assignment all happen server-side.

Closes #100

commit.message:
fix: inline polyfill into entry file to defeat Chromium SW module cache

Chromium caches imported module scripts independently from the main
service worker script. Even when the polyfill file on disk has the
correct port/token, the cached import from a prior session is served.
Fix by inlining the polyfill directly into the entry file so the
port/token are part of the main SW script content, which Chromium
does check for byte changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Honor ignore_patterns in load_workspace_existing_file_node_ids (CP-k870)

The function checked hidden files and allowed extensions but never
applied ignore_patterns from ScanOptions. This caused node IDs from
ignored files to be counted as existing, incorrectly preserving
linked entries that should have been filtered out.

docs(spec): B-30 ✅ HECHO — monorepo desconectado de Railway, deploy via repos independientes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(init): rename branch after first commit; fix(scope): scaffold issue before impl (#254)

* fix(init): rename branch after first commit; fix(scope): scaffold issue before impl

brimstone init was renaming main→mainline before _setup_ci pushed the first
commit, so the branch didn't exist and the rename silently failed. Move the
rename to immediately after _setup_ci.

scope-worker: add mandatory Step 3 scaffold issue (pyproject.toml, __init__.py,
Makefile, README) that all other impl issues depend on. Parallel impl workers
were independently creating these shared files, causing merge conflicts on every
PR.

Closes #253

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(run): use beads as source of truth for stage completion skip gates

Replace GitHub issue count checks with BeadStore reads in the --all loop.
research, design, and scope skip gates now read from the bead store:
- research: skip if research beads exist and none are open/claimed
- design: skip if design beads exist and none are open/claimed, and HLD is merged
- scope: skip if any impl beads exist

make_bead_store is called once per stage check using the outer config;
startup_sequence is not required for skip gate evaluation.

Update unit and integration tests to mock make_bead_store instead of
_count_open_issues_by_label.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(monitor): grace counter for empty CI checks; flush merge queue on resume; scaffold bead pre-flight

Three bugs that prevented conflict-recovered PRs from merging:

1. _get_pr_checks_status returned "pass" for an empty checks list. After a
   force-push, GitHub takes a few seconds to queue CI — an empty list during
   that window was treated as "no CI configured" and the PR was immediately
   enqueued for merge before CI ran. Now returns "no_checks"; _monitor_pr
   waits _CI_NO_CHECKS_GRACE (3) consecutive no_checks polls before treating
   the PR as CI-free and proceeding.

2. _resume_open_prs never called _process_merge_queue. PRs were monitored to
   completion and enqueued, but the queue was never drained during resume —
   merges sat waiting until the next impl worker dispatch loop. Now flushes
   the merge queue after each PR is monitored.

3. Impl pre-flight: _ensure_impl_scaffold() checks for a scaffold issue before
   any agents dispatch. If missing, creates one and wires all other impl beads
   to block on it — guaranteeing pyproject.toml/package init are never created
   by two parallel workers regardless of what the scope agent did.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: restore visited systems from knowledge base on startup

The getUnvisitedNeighbors function was only checking the in-memory
VisitedSystems map, which gets reset when the explorer restarts.
This caused previously visited systems to be shown as "unknown"
even though they existed in the knowledge base with LastUpdatedTick.

Changes:
- Query KB for all systems and LastUpdatedTick at the start
- Restore VisitedSystems map from KB data (LastUpdatedTick > 0)
- Filter out neighbors that exist in KB with non-zero tick
- Show correct "last: tick N" for systems previously visited

This provides persistence across explorer restarts and prevents
re-exploring already-visited systems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: booking_passengers always shows 0 passengers due to broken RLS policies

Two root causes identified:
1. "Agents view assigned passenger data" used auth.jwt() ->> 'role' = 'admin'
   which never works because Supabase does not include the app-level role in
   the JWT token (it always returns 'authenticated'), so admins always got 0 passengers.
2. Policies did subqueries into `bookings` which itself has RLS, causing
   recursive RLS evaluation that could return empty results.

Fix: Created SECURITY DEFINER functions (auth_uid_is_admin,
auth_uid_owns_booking, auth_uid_is_assigned_agent) that bypass RLS
internally, then replaced all broken SELECT policies with a single
correct policy that covers admins, users, and agents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add configurable data URI stripping for inline HTML

Add a document transformer system to the view package and use it to
strip inline HTML tags containing data: URIs (e.g. base64 images) from
parsed documents. Enabled by default via `strip_data_uris` config option.

commit.message:
Fix daemon restart ping-pong between VS Code windows and bump to v0.5.4 (#23)

When two VS Code windows are open and the extension is updated, only
the reloaded window should restart the daemon. Previously any version
mismatch (!=) triggered a restart, causing the stale window to kill
the newer daemon and spawn an old one in a loop. Now only restarts
when the extension is strictly newer than the daemon.

Co-authored-by: Yanxi Chen <yanxichen@yanxic-j0396y2qyc.alpaca-tet.ts.net>
